description: Cross-CLI hook overlay (gh #55). The SSOT for events/matchers/timeouts is each plugin's hooks/hooks.json; this file only selects WHICH hooks each non-Claude CLI consumes, and in what order. Claude Code consumes the full hooks.json union via scripts/sync_hooks_to_live.py. Consumers: install-codex-hooks.sh (codex), install-grok-hooks.sh (grok), harness-kimi/guard-check.sh (kimi), scripts/check_cross_cli_hooks.sh (drift test).

codex.note: Codex consumes the portable subset. String entries must resolve uniquely in the owning hooks.json; object entries select an explicit event when the same command is registered more than once. event/matcher/timeout are looked up from the owning plugin's hooks.json. Order within an event is preserved in the generated TOML.

external.note: hippocampus-mcp companion context injector; lives in the companion repo, not in claude-harness. optional=true → the installer and drift test auto-skip it when that repo is absent, so a standalone claude-harness install ignores it rather than wiring a dead path. ${HIPPOCAMPUS_HOME} defaults to ${HOME}/projects/hippocampus-mcp and can be overridden at install time.

codex-only (deterministic rename + identity inject); intentionally NOT in hooks.json — Claude uses tmux_self_name.sh instead. ${HARNESS_DIR} is expanded to the cloned repo root at install time, so this works wherever the repo lives.

codex-only release automation: after a Bash git push on main, auto-detect semver bump, tag, and create a GitHub Release. Docs-only pushes no-op. Keep the manual versioning skill confirm-gated; this hook is the automation layer.

grok.note: Native Grok hooks via ~/.grok/hooks/harness.json (install-grok-hooks.sh). Bash-shaped PreToolUse/PostToolUse/UserPromptSubmit only; Read/Write matchers deferred to Phase 1.5 (Grok toolInput schema confirmation). Grok maps matcher 'Bash' -> run_terminal_command; event/matcher/timeout are looked up from the owning plugin's hooks.json (same SSOT as codex). Deny shape + payload differences are absorbed by harness-core/hooks/lib.sh (emit_deny/parse_tool_command). Order within an event is preserved.

kimi.note: Bash-command-string guards only (BASH_ENV interception, gh #52) — Kimi has no native hook API, so only PreToolUse/Bash-shaped hooks apply. insurance runs first and never blocks; gates deny with rc=2; hints go to stderr.
